entry.title: Re: Do "address locks" occur for Data [pages] Only Locked (DOL) tables?

entry.title: Re: sh: 1: fiori: not found-> The most popular error for Fiori Developers
entry.content:
In case anyone else doesn't find their answers here, for simple Fiori apps if you haven't installed npm libs and try to run an app you will get the sh: 1: fiori: not found

I had forgotten to do $ npm init in my new dev space in BAS.

entry.title: Re: Inside SAP: Powering HR Self-services with Generative AI at SAP

entry.title: Convert Sapi Source system to ODP source system

entry.title: Write into 'Header Note' text of PR
entry.content:
Hi Peers,

I have a case where I need to write data into HEADER NOTE text area of PR through an Inbound Integration data. any idea how can I achieve this ? I am attaching a screenshot for reference.

 

 

Help Appreciated

Thanks and Regards

entry.title: Re: Facing error when creating MTO Production Order
entry.content:
Hi @yKim 

 

Please follow the KBA  https://me.sap.com/notes/0003366337

 

In SSCUI 102447  Define Checking Control, for the used Order type and plant, the Material Availability check is set as No Check.


Tick off the 'No check' indicator and maintain the checking rule in this SSCUI.

Note : 'No check' means no automatic material availability check is to be executed for the specified business transaction (order creation or order release).
The option of calling up a material availability check manually and also of executing a collective availability check is not affected by setting this indicator.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention Summit: Uniting Thought Leaders for Success
entry.content:
The Summit is scheduled for April 13-15, 2025

We are excited to announce that the SAP MaxAttention Summit is taking place in Munich, Germany, from April 13-15, 2025. This exclusive event is an onsite, in-person gathering designed for the SAP MaxAttention community to engage with thought leaders, SAP experts, and peers from some of the world's leading organizations.

What to expect

From Sunday evening through Tuesday afternoon, the summit will feature captivating keynotes from SAP executives, customer experience sessions, and in-depth expert discussions. In addition, there will be a variety of networking opportunities, including a welcome reception, a special evening event, and one-on-one meetings.

  • Take the opportunity to connect with members of the SAP Executive Board and engage in in-depth expert discussions.
  • Participants will be able to experience SAP MaxAttention in action and learn how leading companies within the SAP MaxAttention community establish their individual North Star enterprise architecture, transition to RISE with SAP, and integrate innovations like SAP Business AI.
  • Moreover, the summit provides you with a chance to connect with a select group of SAP's largest customers, to build relationships, and to gain valuable insights that can take your business to the next level.

Event Location

Welcome Reception (Sunday, April 13, 2025)

Take a look at our booklet 

Check out our booklet including more information around the location, key topics and agenda now. 

Customers eligible to join this exclusive SAP MaxAttention event will be invited personally in January.  

We can't wait to see you in Munich next April for what promises to be an unforgettable experience. Let's come together to learn, connect, and drive success! 

In case of questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

entry.title: Re: HOW IS SPECIAL STOCK INDICATOR DETERMINED IN PP rewor...
entry.content:
Hi Sally.

Please check the settlement profile assigned to the order type which you're using in OPJH. 

And go to t-code OKO7 and check the configuration profile settings. refer the below screen shot, for WBS element it should be 1- Settlement optional.

Thanks & Regards

Ramesh Sigi

entry.title: IBP Excel Add in Editable Key Figure greyed out
entry.content:
Hello!

Normally if Key figure is non-editable it will be greyed out in excel planning view, but if it is editable, color of cells will be white. But now I see that for editable Key figures color of cells is grey.

Although if KF is all editable, it's white, but if it is editable in current of future period, it is grey.

I have administrator rights, so it is for sure not question of permissions.

Also after setting check on the Default Format for Editable Cells in the IBP formatting sheets, it is getting white, but I expect it to be whit by default.

Maybe there is something what I should check in order get cells white for editable cells by default?

On the screenshot KF Approve country DP Short is editable and I can change values there, but color of cells is grey.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports
entry.content:
Hi @Aneta_Krysiak ,

In our instance, we have this in Canvas , Advance Reporting. Check your Foundation Tables. As shared before, we are not seeing this in Story data schema yet :(...hope SF add this in the upcoming future.

I don't know if this is a "standard" configuration or this is something was built for our instance. You can check with your SF support.

entry.title: Re: Updated Learning Journey - Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experienc

Kunal Bansal - L2C Certificate
Thank you once again for sharing.
Best Regards,
Kunal

entry.title: Re: Write into 'Header Note' text of PR
entry.content:
You can use BAPI_PR_CHANGE

  • Header long text to provide in parameter PRHEADERTEXT of BAPI
  • Identify exact key : double-click on text area in ME52N to display text in fullscreen mode, and in the menu navigate: goto, header (ID should be B01, object EBANH)

entry.title: Re: Rejection/ Reminder for RFQ's via Fiori using BRF+ ou...
entry.content:
Hi Samanda,

Please have a look at S/4 HANA standard functionality of situation handling to send reminder emails. 

Use Cases for Situation Handling | SAP Help Portal

There are multiple use cases for sourcing and procurement, just identify which one suits you and see if it can be used.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet C...
entry.content:
Hi Raymond,

Thanks a Lot for your Reply !!

After your suggestion i used transaction SRT_UTIL , applied --> Functional Trace <-- and executed my program and here is the log it shows after refresh : 

-ERROR_MESSAGE  --> Connection to server broken (ICM_HTTP_CONNECTION_BROKEN)

The thing is that roughly 2-3 times out of 10 the program works also and it doesnot give the error but the execution of my original program is not consistent as 80 % of the times it is giving error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet Communication Framework: ("Connection closed") in catch statement. 

What could be the probable solution for this ? 

Best Regards,

Devendra

entry.title: Re: Write into 'Header Note' text of PR
entry.content:
Hi Raymond, I am trying to execute the BAPI_PR_CHANGE from SE37, but I am not getting exactly what values to pass here. 

 

I am passing the PR Number in Number of Import Parameters

 

and I am passing the text in  PRHEADERTEXT table

 

Am I missing anything ?

 

Regards

entry.title: Best practices using SAP_ALL for implementations
entry.content:
Hi there, 

I am currently leading the end-to-end implementation of the embedded EWM module. As we approach the cutover phase in the client’s production environment, I have requested broad access permissions to perform certain client-specific technical activities. These activities often involve complex object configurations that do not follow the typical transport order process. Given these requirements, I believe it is necessary to have a temporary, consultant-level user role with SAP_ALL access. However, the client has raised concerns about audit compliance and is hesitant to grant this access.

Is there any official documentation or best practice guideline available that supports the need for extended permissions during critical implementation phases? This would help the client understand the necessity of SAP_ALL for certain technical tasks during cutover and allow us to proceed effectively

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Inquiry about automatic generation of Material Serial Numbers according to specific rules
entry.content:
We have a question about creating Material Serial Numbers following specific rules for automatic numbering. Currently, I am looking to assign serial numbers based on a custom format. For example, a serial number such as "AA2480011" would indicate that it belongs to the "AA" product group, was produced in August 2024, and is the 11th product manufactured in that series.

Could you please advise if it is possible to configure material serial numbers according to such rules?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Invitation to Beta program for successor to SAP GUI for Windows 8.00?
entry.content:
SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/147519/E  mentioned that "A successor to SAP GUI for Windows 8.00 is currently being developed. The delivery of this next release is not expected before Q3/2025 (this may be subject to change)".

What is the procedure to get invited in the Beta program?

regards

Ulrich Neumann

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about automatic generation of Material Serial...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud there is the BADI 'Internal Serial Number Assignment using Custom Business Objects' to can extend the serial number creation function to create serial numbers automatically in accordance with a custom pattern.

 

Please check the information in the resource Extensibility for Serial Number Creation

 

There is a blog ( https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/implement-custom-serial-numbers-with-an-internal-number-range-using/ba-p/13863879 ) that shows how to implement the BADI with an example

Yes, You are correct I am creating packaging item automatically. 

And I have maintained the TA /SCWM/GMBIN_DET correctly.

Regards,

Please suggest.

entry.title: Re: Best practices using SAP_ALL for implementations
entry.content:
Hello @step158 

The best practice is to never grant SAP_ALL in production, to no one, under no circumstances, no excuses! Sorry, the customer is right.

I don't understand what kind of "complex object configurations that do not follow the typical transport order process" you need to do with SAP_ALL in production. If they are really so complex and are not transportable, then it means your productive system won't be aligned with your test environment. Therefore you won't be able to test reliably. Something is not right here big time.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: reuse HU in SHPCON (WHSCON) IDoc
entry.content: Hi, did you find a solution for this? We are Facing the Same Issue

entry.title: Re: Write into 'Header Note' text of PR
entry.content:
• Pass the requisition number in internal format (leading zeroes)
  • Pass the 'B01', '*' values in text parameter
  • Try to call (test SE37) BAPI_PR_GETDETAIL to get some hint of correct parameter from an existing PR

entry.title: Re: CDS Virtual Element Filter Requirement
entry.content:
I'm facing the same issue: it's a completely no-sense topic.

You found the documentation: "Implementing Filtering for Virtual Elements" (https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/fc4c71aa50014fd1b43721701471913d/9019018679ea491cb55ce7ef66fb6ae4.html?version=201909.002).

thinking there's explained how to apply a filter on a virtual element. Next reading...

"Filtering on virtual elements requires the transformation of the given filter condition into a corresponding condition that does not use any virtual elements for the filter."

"The filter for virtual elements must be implemented in the related ABAP class. For each filter operator, the filtering must be transformed to a filter for the elements from which the virtual element is calculated."

To summarize: to filter a virtual element you have to transform the filter into a filter of non-virtual elements.

Useless.

entry.title: Re: Best practices using SAP_ALL for implementations
IDOC Adapter in SAP CPI- Best Choice between the Content Types
entry.content:
Hi Team,

CPI offers 2 types of Content types for IDoc Adapter.

1. text/xml- that accepts multiple IDocs

2. application/x-sap.idoc - Accepts only single IDoc.

We are trying to build a generic Iflow that connects IDocs to respective SAP system.

what is the best choice to use in our IDoc adapter (application/x-sap.idoc / text/xml)?

entry.title: Re: Message "'UI Update' has encountered a problem." appe...
entry.content: @miha finally I have good news. ☺️ Check my answer below.

entry.title: Re: Rejection/ Reminder for RFQ's via Fiori using BRF+ ou...
entry.content:
Hi 

Yes you are right I think this functionality does not exist. we have a note for Rejection. It advises something about it 

170201 - Rejection output type at S/4Hana output control (a.k.a. BRF+) for RFQs

Symptom
You received Quotation from several vendors for your Request for Quotation document, and you chose the one from which you order the goods, and you want to send rejection letter to the rest of the vendors informing them that the ordering won't happen from their company.
In ME41 RFQ document case at the NAST output logic the output type ABSA was for this purpose.
Is there any similar functinality available for Manage RFQ Fiori app based RFQs and S/4Hana output control (a.k.a BRF+) especially for PDF with fragements output form technology?

Environment
SAP S/4Hana - On-premise

Cause
Missing functionality. There is no special similar PDF with fragements form available yet, so for Fiori app based RFQ documents no such output processing is possible (as in case Manage RFQ Fiori app only the S/4Hana output control, a.k.a. BRF+ logic useable and only the PDF with fragements form type - SAP KBA 2664988).

Development's attention can be also raised if you post a development request as per SAP Note 2967362.

Resolution
As long as the feature not developed, for Fiori app created RFQs rejection mail can not be sent out.
If this kind of feature needed, use ME41 SAP GUI tcode based RFQs and NAST output message ABSA.

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Automatic Start of Production Process
entry.content:
Hi experts,

does anyone of you know if there are any other options to start a production process from production process designer besides a POD or Automatic Triggers?

Thank you!

entry.title: Dive into the World of AI with SAP Learning to Future-Proof Your Career
entry.content:
AI is not just a buzzword but a transformative force across industries. From predictive analytics and automation to enhancing customer experiences and operational efficiencies, AI is reshaping the business world. For companies and professionals eager to stay at the forefront, understanding AI's principles and applications is indispensable. Fortunately, SAP Learning offers an array of resources designed to help you stay ahead of the curve and enhance your professional expertise.

 

 

Why Choose SAP Learning?
SAP Learning provides extensive courses tailored for both beginners and seasoned tech professionals. Join the community of 2 million learners and get insights from industry experts in both practical and theoretical aspects of AI. With flexible online courses designed to fit your busy schedule, you can study at your own pace and deepen your knowledge and skills efficiently.

 

Key Learning Journeys to Explore:

  1. Putting AI Ethics into Practice at SAP: Begin your journey by understanding the importance of AI ethics and learn how to implement ethical standards in AI development. This course ensures you grasp the crucial role of ethical considerations in the deployment of AI technologies.
  2. Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio: Before diving into specific tools, ensure you complete this prerequisite course. It offers essential insights into SAP's AI offerings, laying a solid foundation for further specialized learning.
  3. Provisioning and Implementing Joule: Gain hands-on experience with one of SAP's powerful AI tools. This journey walks you through the entire process of leveraging Joule to streamline business operations, providing practical, real-world skills you can apply immediately.

 

Become Part of the Intelligent Future
The future is intelligent, and being an integral part of this transformation starts with acquiring the right skills. Equip yourself with the knowledge offered on the SAP Learning site. Embrace the AI-driven transformation in the business world and become a valuable asset in any organization's AI endeavors.

 

The future is intelligent. Be a part of it with SAP.
Explore SAP Learning today, and take the first step towards future-proofing your career.

entry.title: Re: Message "'UI Update' has encountered a problem." appe...
entry.content:
Hello solution seeker, 😉

With incredible turn of events I finally managed to solve this issue. Incredible is that ChatGPT suggested me to do the following:

  • Reset the Debug Perspective:

    • Go to Window > Perspective > Reset Perspective.
    • This resets the layout, which might fix any misconfigurations causing the issue.

This worked immediately. 🤯 I think I was still in the debug session and when I start double-clicking on each table and they were displayed in ABAP Internal Table (Debugger) window. The annoying message didn't appeared anymore. 👍

Thanks everyone on the interest. Hope it will work for everyone.

Regards,
Nemanja

entry.title: How Generative AI Can Assist in SAP Fiori Applications: A Practical Demonstration
entry.content:
What if your SAP Fiori applications could anticipate your needs and provide intelligent suggestions to streamline your workflow? With the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence, integrating generative AI models into enterprise applications is no longer a distant future but a present opportunity to enhance efficiency and decision-making.

In this post, I'll share a practical demonstration of how I integrated a generative AI model into an SAP Fiori application using OpenUI5. This solution enhances the creation of maintenance orders by providing AI-generated suggestions based on the order's title and description, helping users avoid overlooking critical steps.

The Solution

I developed a frontend application using OpenUI5, hosted on Vercel, that mirrors a maintenance order request interface in SAP Fiori. The key enhancement is the addition of AI-generated suggestions that appear as the user inputs the maintenance order's title and description.

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

 

 These suggestions offer important steps for performing the task, effectively serving as a smart checklist to assist users in making informed decisions.

Technical Details

Development Environment

  • Frontend: Built with OpenUI5 and deployed on the Vercel platform.
  • Backend: A locally running API utilizing a generative AI model (LLM) to process user inputs and generate suggestions.

Integration with AI

The integration with the generative AI model is achieved through HTTP requests to the local API. When the user enters the maintenance order's title and description, this data is sent to the API, which processes it and returns a list of suggested steps.

 

 

 

 

.route('/infer', methods=['POST'])
def infer():
    """Endpoint para gerar conteúdo com a API do Google."""
    data = request.get_json(force=True)

    if not data or 'prompt' not in data:
        return jsonify({'error': 'No prompt provided'}), 400

    try:
        prompt_parts = [data['prompt']]
        response = model.generate_content(prompt_parts)
        output_text = response.text

    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Erro na inferência: {e}")
        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500

    return jsonify({'output_text': output_text})

 

 

 

Interface Implementation

The user interface is crafted to be intuitive and familiar for SAP Fiori users, which helps to reduce the learning curve. Standard OpenUI5 components are utilized to gather user input and present AI-generated suggestions.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

<!-- Botão "Gerar Sugestões" -->
                <Button id="generateSuggestionsButton" text="Gerar Sugestões" press="onGenerateSuggestions" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />

                <!-- Lista de Passos Sugeridos -->
                <Label text="Passos Sugeridos" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />
                <List id="suggestionsList" width="80%" items="{suggestionsModel>/suggestions}">
                    <StandardListItem title="{suggestionsModel>}" />
                </List>

 

 

 

Demonstration in Action

Here's how the application works in practice:

  1. Title: "Air Filter Replacement"
  2. Description: "The air filter of the compressor needs to be replaced due to particle buildup."

Upon entering this information, the user receives immediate suggestions:

  • 1. Turn off the compressor and disconnect it from the electrical power.
  • 2. Locate the air filter, which is usually in a plastic or metal case.
  • 3. Loosen the screws or clips securing the air filter box.
  • 4. Remove the old air filter and inspect it for dirt and damage.
  • 5. Insert the new air filter into the housing and secure it with the screws or latches.
  • 6. Replace the air filter box on the compressor.
  • 7. Turn on the compressor and check for air leaks.
     
     

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

This practical demonstration highlights the potential of integrating generative AI models into SAP Fiori applications. While this example operates locally without OData communication, it showcases how AI can be leveraged to improve daily tasks and workflows.

 

I'm excited to hear your thoughts! Share your experiences, ideas, and questions in the comments.

Thank you for reading! If you're interested in more technical details or have any questions, feel free to reach out.

SAP Fiori SAP AI Core SAP AI Launchpad  Artificial Intelligence SAP Maintenance Assistant mobile app

entry.title: Key figure for when expected value is 0 - analysis work center C4C
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm trying to create a calculated/restricted key figure that will provide a COUNT of the opportunities WHEN the EXPECTED VALUE is ZERO. I can't put a restriction on a key figure (that I'm aware of) so haven't been able to create a restricted key figure which was my original plan. Does anyone know how this could be achieved?

Thanks in advance. 

Chris

entry.title: Python samples in ASE did not work and fail with an ImportError
entry.content:
Hi

I want to execute the Python samples with Python 3.11.0 or 3.11.8.
Into %SYBASE%\%SYBASE_OCS%\sample\python I copied the SYBASE.bat file from %SYBASE% and add
variables for DSQUERY, PYTHONHOME, PATH, and PYTHONPATH:
REM
REM SAP Product Environment variables
REM
set INCLUDE=C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\include;%INCLUDE%
set LIB=C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\lib;%LIB%
REM
REM Replace dll, lib3p, and lib3p64 with devdll, devlib3p, and devlib3p64 when debugging
REM
set PATH=C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\bin;C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\dll;C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\lib3p64;C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\lib3p;%PATH%
set PATH=C:\SAP\DataAccess\ODBC\dll;%PATH%
set PATH=C:\SAP\DataAccess64\ODBC\dll;%PATH%
set LIB=C:\SAP\DataAccess\ADONET\dll;%LIB%
set PATH=C:\SAP\DataAccess\ADONET\dll;%PATH%
set PATH=C:\SAP\DataAccess64\ADONET\dll;%PATH%
set LIB=C:\SAP\DataAccess64\ADONET\dll;%LIB%
set PATH=C:\SAP\COCKPIT-4\bin;%PATH%
set PATH=C:\SAP\ASE-16_0\bin;C:\SAP\ASE-16_0\dll;C:\SAP\WLA\bin;;%PATH%
set PATH=C:\SAP\ASE-16_0\jobscheduler\bin;%PATH%
set SAP_JRE8_32=C:\SAP\Shared\SAPJRE-8_1_096_32BIT
set SAP_JRE8=C:\SAP\Shared\SAPJRE-8_1_096_64BIT
set SAP_JRE8_64=C:\SAP\Shared\SAPJRE-8_1_096_64BIT
set SYBASE_OCS=OCS-16_0
set COCKPIT_JAVA_HOME=C:\SAP\Shared\SAPJRE-8_1_096_64BIT
set SYBASE=C:\SAP
set SYBASE_ASE=ASE-16_0
set SYBROOT=C:\SAP
set SYBASE_JRE_RTDS=C:\SAP\Shared\SAPJRE-8_1_096_64BIT
set SYBASE_WS=WS-16_0
set DSQUERY=HP450RK
set PYTHONHOME=C:\Program Files\Python311
set PATH=%PYTHONHOME%;%PATH%
set PYTHONPATH=%SYBASE%\%SYBASE_OCS%\python\python311_64\dll

I navigate to the %SYBASE%/%SYBASE_OCS%/sample/python directory and execute SYBASE.bat
for setting the environment. Executing results in the following error:

c:\SAP\OCS-16_0\sample\python>python %SYBASE%\%SYBASE_OCS%\sample\python\test.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "C:\SAP\OCS-16_0\sample\python\test.py", line 53, in <module>
import sybpydb
ImportError: DLL load failed while importing sybpydb: Das angegebene Modul wurde nicht gefunden.

The translation of the ImportError is "The specified module was not found."
I tried Python 3.11.0 and 3.11.8 installations for me only and for all users of the system. The errormessage
is allways the same.
What are I'm doing wrong?

Many thanks

Robert

entry.title: Re: statusCode: 400, Response: Multiple emails not suppor...
entry.content:
Hi Sjorge,

Below notes may help you to troubshoot the issue:

  • If the replaceLastString function isn’t working as expected, try using IAS’s other available functions. Alternatively, break down the transformation into smaller steps, replacing the domain in separate transformations to verify each part works as intended.
  • Since $.emails[*].value captures all emails, ensure the JSON path is capturing the expected array structure. If yiu have only one email, preserveArrayWithSingleElement: true should maintain it as an array, but it's worth verifying the JSON path output.
  • Make sure the condition syntax is correct and matches the actual data format. IAS transformations are sensitive to exact matches, so you might want to double-check that $.emails[0].value has exactly @olddomain.com for applicable entries. Try to test within IAS to see if the condition returns the expected results.
  • Confirm the regex in replaceLastString is functioning as expected. For example, .*@olddomain.com should work, but if IAS has any issues with regex syntax, you may need to try alternative patterns or escape characters.
  • Look at IAS logs for errors related to this specific transformation. IAS logs can sometimes provide additional information about why a transformation fails.

LINKS:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-user-identifier-attributes
https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/transformation-expressions

Hope this helps,

@Amin_Omidy

entry.title: Re: How to upload the bulk data in S/4HANA Public Cloud?S...
entry.content: Hi Jerry

entry.title: How to Retrigger Output Parameter Determination Automatically Based on Field Update in Sales Order
entry.content:
We are using Output Parameter Determination (OPD) to send an order confirmation when a specific field is populated in the Sales Order. However, this field is typically updated some time after the initial document creation. Currently, it seems that OPD only executes at the time of Sales Order creation. At that point, the field is not yet populated, so the output is either not triggered, or it fails to meet the output relevance criteria. But when the field is updated, OPD does not re-evaluate, and the output is never triggered or sent. However, When we configure the output without the specific field condition (or any other field), it is triggered successfully. So the retriggering must be the problem.

Is there a way to automatically retrigger OPD based on the field update? Is there potentially another solution to ensure output only triggers when this specific field is populated? We have thoroughly tested different options regarding the determination steps for output type and output relevance, but without success. 

Kind regards, Tim

entry.title: How to Remove a measure from a chart in sac script
entry.content:
Hi @N1kh1l 

add measure is working but remove measure is not working. Even i used remove member also, but when i change the list measure is not removed form the chart

if(ListBox_1.getSelectedKey()==="PickUnits")
{
Chart_25.setVisible(false);
 
 
Chart_25.addMeasure("ELTUIDCMP7",Feed.ValueAxis);
Chart_25.addMeasure("33558012-7484-4421-3006-224195070345",Feed.ValueAxis2);
 
Chart_25.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"ELTUIDCMP2");
Chart_25.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis2,"31948283-6646-4859-3131-200104570027");
 
Chart_25.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"ELTUIDCMP3");
Chart_25.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis2,"70672825-9386-4722-8931-171337372722");
 
 
}
 
if(ListBox_1.getSelectedKey()==="PickSLots")
{
 
 
Chart_25.addMeasure("ELTUIDCMP2",Feed.ValueAxis);
Chart_25.addMeasure("31948283-6646-4859-3131-200104570027",Feed.ValueAxis2);
 
Chart_25.removeMeasure("ELTUIDCMP7",Feed.ValueAxis);
Chart_25.removeMeasure("33558012-7484-4421-3006-224195070345",Feed.ValueAxis2);
 
 
Chart_25.removeMeasure("ELTUIDCMP3",Feed.ValueAxis);
Chart_25.removeMeasure("70672825-9386-4722-8931-171337372722",Feed.ValueAxis2);
 
//console.log(Chart_25);
}
 
Regards,
Thayu.

entry.title: Custom mandatory comment in Compensation Form
entry.content:
Hello,

A client is requesting to create a mandatory comment (displayed as a pop-up free text zone) in compensation form.

The conditions are whenever two custom "string, editable" fields are not empty "New Job" and/or "New Classification" the Planner is asked to justify his entry.

How can I configure this request?

Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: Best practices using SAP_ALL for implementations
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Crystal Report Change Text interpretation runtime is not working for database field
entry.content:
I am trying to change the field's text interpretation to HTML at runtime but it isn't working on database field. It is working for formula field. Can anyone help?

CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportDefModel.FieldObject fldObject = rptObj ; CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportDefModel.FieldObject newClone = fldObject.Clone(true); newClone.FieldFormat.StringFormat.TextFormat = CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ReportDefModel.CrTextFormatEnum.crTextFormatHTMLText;   //this is not working newClone.Format.HorizontalAlignment = CrAlignmentEnum.crAlignmentRight;  //this is working

reportDefController.ReportObjectController.Modify(fldObject, newClone);

entry.content:
Hello:

I had the same issue, and I have read the Venkat's comment about CALCTYPCON and it works, you can see the permit values in the detailed view in the condition record in the sales order.  I put it a 'B' for Fixed value.  Maybe it was late but, it could help anyone else.

Thanks Venkat

and Python 3.11 instead of 3.1 (given in the document). As ASE 16.0.4 PL06 has an directory
"python311_64" I hope that Python 3.11.x is supported.

Robert

entry.title: Re: How is the 'Accuracy - Current Period' field calculated in the 'Maintain PIRs' app?
entry.content: Actually if you add consumption mode then Accuracy = (Withdrawn quantity + PIR) / Demand * 100

entry.title: Re: How to upload the bulk data in S/4HANA Public Cloud?S...
entry.content: Hi Hepsibha, By chance did you check the link I provided for Import Sales Orders? The app has a "download template" button - it is an excel file. With that app, you fill the excel file with the data and then you can upload the file via the app. Each line is considered a sales order creation request. The Help also describes the procedure. Thank you Jerry

entry.title: Re: Partial Service Order Confirmation

entry.title: Re: The Procurement Monthly - November 2024
entry.content: Thanks, Gordon, for taking the time and effort to summarize the latest and most significant market reports every month.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Element List Report - Adjust column size automatically
entry.content:
Great blog. Really helped me out.

In case anyone's doing this for an Analytical Page, here's the controller code that worked for me:

 

sap.ui.controller("ca.gc.aafc.eec_alp.ext.controller.AnalyticalListPageExt", {

    onInit: function (oEvent) {
        if (!this._sIdPrefix) {
            this._sIdPrefix =
                "<namespace+appname>::sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage.view.AnalyticalListPage::EEC_AlpTotalsSet--"; //analyticalTable-selall

        }

    },

    onAfterRendering: function (oEvent) {
        var oContentTable = this.byId(this._sIdPrefix + "analyticalTable");
        oContentTable.attachBusyStateChanged(this._onBusyStateChanged);

    },
    _onBusyStateChanged: function (oEvent) {
        var bBusy = oEvent.getParameter("busy");
        if (!bBusy && !this._bColumnOptimizationDone) {
            var oTable = oEvent.getSource();
            var oTpc = null;
            if (sap.ui.table.TablePointerExtension) {
                oTpc = new sap.ui.table.TablePointerExtension(oTable);
            } else {
                oTpc = new sap.ui.table.extensions.Pointer(oTable);
            }
            var aColumns = oTable.getColumns();
            for (var i = aColumns.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
                //oTpc.doAutoResizeColumn(i);  didn't work!!
                oTable.autoResizeColumn(i);
                //aColumns[i].autoResize();  //rsh
            }
            //This line can be commented if you want the columns to be adjusted on every scroll
            this._bColumnOptimizationDone = true;
        }

    }

entry.title: SAP B1 Common Functions Disappearing
entry.content:
Hi,
I am really not sure where to post questions about SAP Business One.  If someone can help that would be appreciated.

For this one, I have users that are losing the shortcuts in the Common Functions on the dashboard.  Is there something that I can do to resolve this?

entry.title: Build apps nested composite components errors since runtime 4.12.132
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

Did anyone else experience any issues with nesting two or more custom build composite components in SAP Build Apps recently? Platform: web deployment.

 

So some more explanation:

In SAP Build Apps, developers can create custom components with internal logic, private variables, and properties. These components can also trigger events and expose data to their parent components. Previously, it was possible to nest custom components without issues: events triggered by a nested component would propagate correctly, and private variables within each component would remain isolated.

Issue Overview:

Since a recent update (runtime: 4.12.132), this behavior has changed. Now, when a nested custom component triggers an event to its root custom component, the root component’s private variables are unexpectedly reset. This disrupts the internal logic, leading to inconsistencies in functionality and data handling.

Is this a bug, or is it an intentional change in behavior that we should adjust our implementations for? Any insights or plans for addressing this would be highly appreciated!

We have realised that avoiding nested custom components and relying on 'primitive' components within one custom components seems to resolve the issue, however, it seems to not be the best way of doing component-based UI. There was no such issue on the previous runtime that we built our web app on: 4.12.127.

Looking forward to your insight.

PS for visual people just a simple graphic:

entry.title: Re: reuse HU in SHPCON (WHSCON) IDoc
entry.content: Marcus,

entry.title: Customer credit control issue
entry.content:
Hello,

We are on S4HANA, We have an issue with customer credit control since last SPS update.

Control and blocking is not done when creating new sales order for customer who credit exposure is not yet exceeding credit limit (case1)
but if customer credit exposure already exceed credit limit, all new sales orders will be blocked correctly (case2)

It seems that during creation of sales order the credit check done without consider the new credit Exposure of customer, including the amount of sales order created.

We have the same problem for a second company on the same system who have control when creating deliveries.

Could you please give us some analysis to identify the origin of the problem.

Thanks.

entry.title: API Testing in SAP Build Actions Dropping Part of Request
entry.content:
Hi Builders,

I'm testing the SAP Ariba API "Create Procurement Workspace" in the Actions tab of the SAP Build platform before deploying it in a Build Process Automation flow. I've noticed that when I try to provide a value for the "FieldValueMap" field, this value is dropped from the API response body and is not in the Procurement Workspace that is created.  I have tested this request on the API development platform Bruno and it successfully picked up the value provided in this field which makes me think it's more of an Actions issue than an API request issue.  Any help as to what may be happening would be much appreciated!  Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud ALM for Integration Suite
entry.content: Sorry, but system Says: "The endpoint creation is not required for the SAP Integration Suite, as none of the Cloud ALM use cases requires an endpoint."

entry.title: Professional Service for billing the product P003 is not allocated for usage based billing
entry.content:
Dear SAP Professional Services community,
APP:  Plan Customer Projects

I choose with the above APP and project to customer the work package = Service based (unit) and the Service unit = product / Activty type = U001. The related sales price for product U001 (PCP0 - Project WBS) = 1,15 € / per unit (in total 10.000 unit for the 6 months contract period). After project stage release and on the billing tab I used the contract type = usage based and after enter the system automated allocate the product = P002 instead of P003. 

Furthermore I can't create the monthly billing with the monthly unit = 500 and sales unit price = 1,15 € for the amount to be billed = 575 € (revenue).

I hope you could support me in this regards

Michael

entry.title: Re: PDF Attachment in VA01
entry.content:
I was facing the same issue. I could resolve it by maintaining below parameter in user profile. 

SD_GOS_ATTACHMENTS = X

Please refer SAP Note 1466810 - Object services missing in SD Sales transactions - SAP for Me for more details.

entry.title: Re: Custom mandatory comment in Compensation Form
entry.content:
Hi @MDI8 

forced comments are only available through standard recommendation columns at this time so the exact requirement you are describing cannot be met.

You could however look at building a Hard stop using custom validations to prevent a Save (but it wouldn't come with comments). example here.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: reuse HU in SHPCON (WHSCON) IDoc
entry.content:
In the standard, the system checks the uniqueness flag set in customizing with function module HU_TVSHP_SELECT.

The enhancement you need to implement aim to temporary consider that variable ef_exidv_unique = 0 (uniqueness flag deactivated) to avoid the triggering of the standard error message.

To avoid side effect of the complete Uniqueness deactivation, this deactivation is required only when it is really required :
- when GI is posted against a SHPCON IDoc
- or in case of a manual VL09 cancellation flow of a delivery which was previously sent to an external system because other wise you will have an inconsistency

For the first case :

In our solution SHPCON IDoc are processed with a specific function module ZFM_IDOC_INPUT_DELVRY.

If this function module is triggered, we save in the memory buffer a boolean value = True. We call this value in the enhancement of function module HU_TVSHP_SELECT to proceed, if it is equal to TRUE, with the deactivation of HU uniqueness flag.

For the second case:

The goods issue cancellation flows in SAP uses the standard function group SAPLV51W. Include LV51WFHU contains the functions which are used to cancel goods issue on handling units. At this point, we’re able to retrieve the outbound delivery number which is not directly available in function module HU_TVSHP_SELECT
In the standard, the system checks the uniqueness flag set in customizing with function module HU_TVSHP_SELECT.

In your enhancement :
- you need to check if the transaction used is VL09 (sy-tcode = ‘VL09’) 
- you also need to check if an output has already been sent to an external system: use the delivery number to look up for an entry in table NAST (log table for output) with partner type = LS. If there is at least an entry, you can save the delivery number in the memory buffer and proceed with the enhancement.

entry.title: Re: Leverage the SAP Analytics Cloud Data Export Service to extract your planning data to SAP HANA,
entry.content:
Moin Max,

thanks for your helpfull blog: Please allow me one question, can you tell us, if this is your recommended and strategic future plan using SAP SDI for (full data) SAC Writeback connections?

If you have some time, this is a closer view to our background:

Right now, we (BWonHANA 7.5; BPC 10.1 Standard; SAC Reporting; and upcoming in 01.2025 SAC Planning) are not using SAP SDI. For externel SAP BW APIs with SaaS-Origin we are using SAP BTP IS. For internal origin we are fine using also SAP SDA Connections.

Using SDI for only getting Master Data (in the end also via ODATA) in a low range is a another investment to do, beside the huge BTP invest. As you know, we have to setup Servers, pay for Linux licences and security talkings, because it is a new way in from outside to our OnPremise platform – next to the prefered BTP IS solutions. With SDI/DP-Agent this would be the third technical solution beside BTP (Non SAP SaaS) and CLC-Connections (SAC Live, SAC ODATA Legacy).

We found nothing in this direction on your roadmaps, either how it will works with the upoming „seamless planning“. We are interested in using SAP Datasphere with SAC Plannning in future planning scenarios. And also here we are questioing ourself, is SDI  the right way to go with?

I know, it's a tough question, but maybe you have some insights from the SAP laboratory. Can you confirm, that SAP BTP is not your supported option to consume an ODATA-Connection from SAC Planning and SDI is the solution for all your future planning sceanrios in BW/4 and Datasphere?

Thanks in advance for your help.

Best Regards from Hamburg,

Torsten

entry.title: Visibility of custom fields for end users
entry.content:
Hi Veryone,

 

There is a way to make custom fields of the item entity visible for the end users?

 

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Visibility of all the status of the approvals?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

As a learning administrator in the user file in the approval tab I can see all the approvals with all statutes (denied, approved, cancelled...).

It seems that for the end user, he can only see the pending approvals.

There is a way for the end user to see all the statutes as well (denied, approved, cancelled...)?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Shared row axis in two tables in SAP Analytics Cloud...
entry.content: Thank you William!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Writing formatted text to a file.
entry.content:
I'm investigation ways of presenting formatted information from the results of say an LLM call.

I've looked into the Word SDK and this has an Insert text (Word) action. However, this has a single input of text which won't show for example html formatting in the word document.

The best we can do is open a pre-existing word document as a 'tempate' which has a formatted header, we set our output string to the body of the document which will have a format of whatever is specified in the template doc (Heading 3 in this case).

This is OK, but  it would be much better if we had a mechanism for passing in formatted text. That way we could have a document with headings in bold, different colours, and whatever styling we choose, making for a more professional-looking report. At the moment we can have line breaks and that's about it.

 

Are there any alternative to the Word SDK? I've also looked into the Microsoft office 365 Cloud SDK which handles SharePoint files but a) that doesn't seem to have a write formatted text action either and b) we are awaiting Azure access to get this setup.

entry.title: Re: Third-party process overview
entry.content:
I have a scenario where same vendor-material combination is used for direct procurement (shipping to our warehouse) and also for 3rd party (shipping directly to customer). We maintain PIR where the confirmation control is set to "004". This works for direct procurement as we post goods receipt against inbound delivery document. However, in 3rd party PO (different doc. type) also the confirmation control is defaulting to "004" and user has to manually remove it from the PO to post goods receipt directly against the PO. 

Is there a way to stop the confirmation control from defaulting in 3rd party PO? 

Any insights will be appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @DobrovolskayaTanya 

the service destination can be created with the following command:
cf create-service destination lite python_dest_service

 

During a recent deployment I also had the issue with "datasphere not found". The issue was that the CLI command was not found or the path not set correctly. For a quick fix I added in the execution of the CLI commands the following path:

Hope this helps!

Regards

Christian

entry.title: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" function - HUPAST
entry.content:
Hello,

I've created a flavor on HUPAST where my user can scan the EAN number of a product instead of the product.

I've made the script to filled automatically the material and quantity, and the script should stop here.

When I execute the script manually, it works fine and stop on the quantity field.

When I put the script on the OnEnter event, the script runs and pack automatically the quantity without stopping.

I debug it and cannot figure out why it doesn't stop.

I'm on personas SP18 / ECC6 EHP7.

Thanks and best regards,

Emmanuel.

entry.title: Re: Defaulting to the legacy home page - H2 2024 (New Lea...
entry.content: @PL1: In 2H 2024 SAP removes the switch to activate/deactivate the ILX. It will be active from then on.

entry.title: SAP internship in cybersecurity
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am looking for a long-term internship for the second year of my master's program in Cybersecurity, which will be next year. If you have any information about long-term internship opportunities in SAP for cybersecurity students or anything you can help me with, please do not hesitate to reply.

Thank you.

entry.title: S/4HANA product Compliance - NEW Dangerous Goods Management Class 7 support
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I have recently become an active member on this community forum as I am exploring more about the New S/4HANA Product Compliance solution's capability and assess its suitability for my customer. As part of the overall project, we will be implementing S/4HANA 2023 - this is non-negotiable.

I am seeking advice in terms of the Dangerous Goods Management solution and its support for Class 7. I have (very recently) managed to set up the solution on our demo system and have a very good idea of how this process works on a generic level. Steps I performed as follows: 

  1. (pre requisite) - activate relevant dangerous goods compliance requirements, e.g. IATA, IMDG, ADR, and assign to a compliance purpose
  2. material master records (that represent the unpackaged and packaged product) set to compliance relevant. These are called product master in S/4HANA Fiori
  3. create product compliance view for unpackaged product - assign the material master records to the view. Maintain relevant compliance data in the view
  4. assign compliance purposes from step 0 to the compliance view
  5. classify unpackaged product - assign relevant UN number per regulation. This determines the relevant descriptions
  6. classify packaged product - assign relevant UN number per regulation, Determine enclosure variants and packaging. assess transport permissibility

I now have some questions 🙂

As mentioned before, we are specifically interested in classifying products under Class 7 regulations (such as UN 2977 and UN 2978).

  1. The customer completes their classification of items on a batch-by-batch basis e.g. at delivery rather than at master data level. If that "packaged product" contains less quantity of the unpackaged material, then it may be classified under a different number. Further, it may have different enclosures depending on what unpackaged material is inside the packaged product. Finally, the dangerous goods note will have to have varied data on it depending on the material and qty in the packaged product. What would be the correct way of managing this? 
  2. Following from point 1, the quantity of unpackaged material and the weight of the overall packaged product must be declared, which would only be made available at transactions such as delivery (e.g. handling unit). Further, other class 7 relevant information such as the transport index, activity and critical safety index is not available in the dangerous goods classification app, and logically can only be entered at point of delivery anyway. Entering these details would then determine further labelling such as Radioactive Category I, Category II White/Yellow etc. which can’t really be determined at the master data level, such as the product compliance view. I have noticed the transport index (T.I.), packaging instruction, critical safety index (C.S.I), and radioactive category I/II/III labels calculated from the T.I. are available in the Handling Unit screen in the VL03N transaction. Can these be incorporated in the standard solution, and if so, how?
  3. It is unclear to me if this solution in S/4HANA 2023 actually generates a dangerous goods note as standard or not. The roadmap I reviewed suggests to me that the generation of dangerous goods notes is unavailable. Please confirm if my assumption is correct. If so, this would mean that we will require an enhancement to print a customised form.
  4. Can the same material representing a packaged product be assigned to multiple different materials representing unpackaged products? We have a scenario where a parent material representing a container will have different child materials that represent different compositions and concentrations. 

Any information with support for Class 7 and ability to classify at batch/serialised level will be fantastic.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Defaulting to the legacy home page - H2 2024 (New Lea...
entry.content: @PL1: In 2H 2024 SAP removes the switch to activate/deactivate the ILX. It will be active from then on. Only the second switch (New Learning Experience switch default setting) remains. But this one only determines which homepage version the user will see when he enters the LMS. Is is not possible to remove the toggle for him, so that he cannot switch to ILX. But that is exactly what we need because the ILX still has missing important functions and UX bugs!

entry.title: Re: the exchange rate is showing wrong at the Header leve...
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

My client has many users who have no knowledge about exchange rates. Is it possible to input automatically?

entry.title: The future of process mining is knocking: Join the AI-assisted process analyzer beta program
entry.content:
On November 6th we’ve announced the release of the SAP Signavio, AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights capability. Previously available for testing to select few customers, this capability is now accessible to all SAP Signavio Process Intelligence customers via an open beta program.

This blog delivers essential information on this new AI capability and how you can be among the first to experience its features by joining our dedicated beta program.

AI-assisted process analyzer, text to insights – what is it

Process mining is vital for any organization but is often regarded as a field reserved to experts who are familiar with data analysis and complex query language. This new AI capability changes that perspective. It is designed to democratize process mining, making it accessible to a broader range of stakeholders. Regardless of their technical expertise, a wider range of users, including business users, can use natural language to obtain deep and immediate insights that inform strategic and business decisions.

Check out this short interactive demo that gives a sneak peek into the capability.  

About the beta program 

This open beta program provides you with early access to the process analyzer capability enabling you to test its features and experience how these support your business requirements.​ 

Why participating 

By joining our beta program, you will have the opportunity to:   

  • Access Early Innovation: Gain early access to our new AI-assisted process analyzer and test this capability with your own processes, data, and landscape before its official release.  
  • Shape the Future: Provide valuable feedback on features, usability, and quality, helping us refine and perfect this innovative capability.   
  • Stay Ahead: Obtain early access to additional process analyzer features scheduled for release in early 2025.    

Participation requirements: The beta program is free of charge and open to all SAP Signavio Process Intelligence customers with an active license.  

Timelines: Registration period has started on November 6th and ends on May 31st, 2025. The program duration is minimum 180 days from signing the TEA (Test and Evaluation Agreement), on a first-come, first-served basis.  

Seats are limited, so act fast! Contact your customer success partner or account manager to apply or write me directly here.

Don't miss this chance to be part of SAP Signavio's latest AI innovation. Join us in shaping the future of process intelligence!  

For more information about our process AI capabilities, visit this page.

entry.title: How the proposed PIR is calculated in Maintain PIR
entry.content: How this proposed PIR is calculated

entry.title: python script that uses oauth2.0 with 'api access' without manuel login to fetch data via odata?
entry.content:
Python script to get Datasphere data via odata api:

I have seen some guides to create oauth2.0 client and make rest calls to datasphere odata apis. However, that  oauth2.0 client uses the 'interactive' purpose with a callback uri. That means i have to manually login on browser to get the first token. Later use that to generate the access_token.

However, I tried with a oauthclient2.0 with 'api access' purpose and 'client_credentials'  as authorization, in order to avoid manual login. But the token does not work.. It does not retrieve any data rather it gives as a response text the login page html content.  
So my question is it possible to somehow avoid maneul login and run a python script to get data from Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: Is there any ETA on the Phase II Release yet!
